summary: DHH reflects on discovering an old newspaper clipping about Konsollen, an online gaming magazine he edited at age 18, seven years prior. He reminisces about running a team of nine writers across Denmark to produce a free online publication. The post is a brief, nostalgic look back at his first venture into internet publishing and gaming journalism. He expresses pride in the project and surprise at how quickly time has passed.
key_points: DHH started in internet publishing at 18 as editor of Konsollen, a free online gaming magazine
He managed a distributed team of nine writers across the country, foreshadowing his later remote-work advocacy
The project launched his career trajectory from gaming journalist to tech entrepreneur
money_quotes: I ran a crew of nine writers spread out throughout all over the country and delivered a free online magazine that I'm very proud of to this day.
It was my first venture into internet publishing and the start of a career as a gaming journalist.
key_insight: DHH's earliest ventures already showed the pattern of distributed team leadership and internet publishing that would define his career.
